name: Plank
resistance_profile: Anti-extension core stability hold; trains bracing more than growth.
execution_steps: Set your forearms on the floor directly under your shoulders, elbows bent about 90 degrees.
Extend your legs behind you and rise onto your toes.
Form one rigid straight line from head to heels, with no sagging or piking at the hips.
Brace your abs and squeeze your glutes, breathe steadily, and hold for time.
good_when: You're a beginner learning to brace and stabilize the core.
You want a no-equipment core exercise you can do anywhere.
You need a low-back-friendly anti-extension drill.
bad_when: You're chasing ab muscle growth (use loaded crunches or leg raises).
You can already hold 60-plus seconds easily (progress to a weighted or harder variation).
You want to train rotation or the lateral core (use a side plank or Pallof press).
